response:
Note: This is in an alternate universe. This shop, despite being awesome, never existed. It does in this alternate universe. We’ve loved being in this spot as part of the wonderful community. Unfortunately, this shop is now closed. The realities of retail in the shopping landscape have made this decision unavoidable. We want to take the opportunity to thank you for shopping with us for so many years. You can find all about the cartoon and its related online world at a fictional website. Goodbye forever… This sign was posted to the doors of a children’s shop in the city on Wednesday. The image ends with a poignant “Goodbye forever…” above an image of a crying character. The store closed on Wednesday after 12 years trading on a famous street. The shop sold merchandise related to the animated series. The cartoon is a show about circular colorful animals and their fun adventures. The shop opened in 1995 and was originally a general toy shop, but in 2003 it became focused on the then new surge in the show’s popularity in the region. However, in June 2007, the store closed for the last time. The group’s reasoning was that “the realities of retail” was unavoidable. The shop was also closed so the company could focus on business in its native country and develop more exciting new animated series or movies. 

A local resident posted on 23 June 2007: It’s the end of an era, even bigger than the end of a famous show, or even a well-known actor leaving a role. The famous shop has gone. After about 12 years of selling toys and 4 years of its golden age of merchandise, the store has unfortunately vanished, in its place is a rather upsetting image of the character crying. It pains my heart to see the poor guy like this. Apparently, “the realities of retail in the shopping landscape have made this change unavoidable.” What’s most striking is “Goodbye forever…” written in a melancholic font, and the most striking part is that it’s showing the character crying. My kids will miss the shop dearly. There is another sign giving the channel numbers for a TV channel that broadcasts the cartoon. Although there is an error, a former company doesn’t exist anymore, it's now another company. 

Another resident posted on 23 June 2007: It’s a very sad state of affairs that the shop has ceased to exist. A good shop. Now we will have to
